mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate increases above 1.5% signal monetary tightening that raises discount rates on equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. The mechanism operates through direct repricing of terminal value in discounted cash flow models.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS was 0.41% as of mid-May 2022, indicating early tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 1.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) of more than 100,000 persons signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending capacity and corporate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as forward-looking equity markets reprice growth.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 100,000 persons month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS was 145,128 thousand as of mid-May 2022
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the correlation between PAYEMS monthly change and 5-day forward returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in real retail sales (RSAFS) over a 3-month rolling window indicates weakening consumer demand and reduced corporate revenue growth expectations. This triggers negative equity repricing over the next 60 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines by more than 2% over 3-month rolling window
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS was 465,513 million dollars as of mid-May 2022
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following RSAFS declines exceed baseline returns by more than 1% (t-test p > 0.10), or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.5% month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 8% year-over-year, signaling stagflation conditions. This joint squeeze on margins produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as profit expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly growth < 0.5% AND CPIAUCSL year-over-year > 8%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO was 103.86 and CPIAUCSL was 242.35 as of mid-May 2022
regime_hint: stagflation regime with supply constraints and demand persistence
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of stagflation-triggered instances, or the interaction term (INDPRO deceleration × CPIAUCSL elevation) shows Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline below 1,200 thousand units annualized while unemployment (UNRATE) rises above 4.5%, signaling synchronized demand and labor market weakness. This dual deterioration produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as recession probability increases in market pricing.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST was 1,323 thousand and UNRATE was 4.6% as of mid-May 2022
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with emerging recession signals
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of dual-weakness triggered instances, or the joint signal shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation-adjusted real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL year-over-year change) turns positive and exceeds 1.0% in real terms, signaling restrictive monetary policy stance. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the equity risk premium expands to compensate for higher real rates.
candidate_trigger: Real FEDFUNDS (nominal minus 12-month CPI change) exceeds 1.0%
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.41% and CPIAUCSL at 242.35 as of mid-May 2022
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances when real rates exceed 1.0%, or the real rate signal shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) remains below 4.5% while payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100,000 persons per month, indicating tight labor market with slowing hiring momentum. This signals wage pressure and reduced hiring capacity, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as margin compression expectations increase.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.5% AND PAYEMS monthly growth < 100,000 persons
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 4.6% and PAYEMS was 145,128 thousand as of mid-May 2022
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market with wage pressure and hiring deceleration
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of tight-but-slowing labor market instances, or the interaction signal shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized while real retail sales (RSAFS) decline month-over-month, signaling demand-side recession risk. This produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as equity markets reprice terminal growth rates and recession probability.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth < 1.5% AND RSAFS declines month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 was 16,712.5 billion (chained 2017 dollars) and RSAFS was 465,513 million as of mid-May 2022
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion transitioning to demand-driven slowdown
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in more than 60% of demand-weakness triggered instances, or the joint signal shows Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.
